summary: Reference context is fundamentally sound — the fix location (hir_to_mir.rs lower_top_level) and the chosen spec to modify (hir-to-mir.md) are correct and well-supported by code evidence. Two medium-severity issues need correction: (1) spec_plan sections include the non-standard 'logic' label not used anywhere in the project's change spec format; (2) compiler-driver.md is rated HIGH relevance but the fix is in the lowering layer, not the driver — it should be MEDIUM. Two low-severity observations: resolve/name-resolution.md's key requirements cite code observations rather than spec requirements, and hir-to-mir.md currently has no R covering __name__ initialization (a new requirement must be added, not just a modification to existing ones).

checklist_results.item: All affected crates/areas from pre-clarifications are covered by at least one spec
checklist_results.note: Pre-clarification says fix is in module initialization before executing the module body. The chosen spec hir-to-mir.md covers hir_to_mir.rs where lower_top_level() lives — the right place.

checklist_results.item: Relevance scores are reasonable (high = directly implements, medium = related, low = background)
checklist_results.note: compiler-driver.md is rated HIGH but the fix is entirely in the lowering layer (lower_top_level in hir_to_mir.rs), not in the driver. CompilerSession::run() calls the lowering pass but does not itself implement __name__ initialization. HIGH implies directly implements — this spec should be MEDIUM.

checklist_results.item: Key requirements listed per spec are accurate (match actual requirement IDs)
checklist_results.note: Most key requirements are accurate: hir-to-mir line references (3007-3013) verified, module.md R5 verified, value-and-rc R1 NaN-boxing explanation verified. resolve/name-resolution.md requirements are code-level observations rather than spec IDs, but the underlying facts are correct.
All 6 specs are at least marginally relevant to the bug and fix path.

checklist_results.item: spec_plan: every entry has main_spec_ref set (not null)
spec_plan: sections are reasonable for the requirements
checklist_results.note: 'logic' is not a standard section in the CCLAB change spec format. Existing change specs in this project (e.g. the lower/hir-to-mir no-arg-constructor change spec) use: Overview, Requirements, Scenarios, Diagrams, Test Plan, Changes. The section should be 'requirements' or 'scenarios', not 'logic'.

checklist_results.item: spec_plan: modify entries have valid source paths
spec_plan: main_spec_ref paths include a subfolder (not root-level under crate)
spec_plan: each spec file covers exactly one logical unit (not multiple unrelated concerns)
checklist_results.note: hir-to-mir.md covers HIR→MIR lowering as a single logical unit. Adding __name__ initialization (a module preamble injection in the same source file) is consistent with this scope.

checklist_results.item: spec_plan: no spec file would require duplicate section types (split into separate files if needed)
spec_plan: spec paths mirror source structure (interfaces/, logic/, generate/)

issues.description: spec_plan sections contain non-standard 'logic' label. The sections listed are 'overview, logic, changes, test-plan'. 'logic' does not exist in the CCLAB change spec format. The existing same-file change spec at cclab/specs/crates/cclab-mamba/lower/hir-to-mir (no-arg-constructor fix) uses: Overview, Requirements, Scenarios, Diagrams, API Spec, Test Plan, Changes.
issues.recommendation: Replace 'logic' with 'requirements' (for the new R covering __name__ StoreGlobal injection in lower_top_level) and add 'scenarios' for the test case. Final sections: overview, requirements, scenarios, changes, test-plan.

issues.description: compiler-driver.md is rated HIGH relevance but the fix does not touch the driver. HIGH means 'directly implements'; the driver only orchestrates the call to lower_hir_to_mir_with_symbols(). The actual __name__ injection lives entirely in lower_top_level() inside hir_to_mir.rs. The driver spec has no spec_plan entry, which is consistent with the fix not being there — but the HIGH rating contradicts that.
issues.recommendation: Downgrade compiler-driver.md relevance from HIGH to MEDIUM. Keep it in the context table since it provides useful pipeline context, but the rating should reflect that the fix is in the lowering layer.

issues.description: resolve/name-resolution.md key requirements cite code-level observations, not spec requirements. The listed requirements ('VariableClass::Local is the classification for __name__ when never assigned by user code', 'VarKind classification determines whether LoadGlobal or local VReg is used') are observations about hir_to_mir.rs lines 2994-3013, not formal requirements from the name-resolution.md spec. The spec's actual requirements (R1-R5) cover global/nonlocal statement semantics, not __name__-specific VariableClass behavior.
issues.recommendation: Rephrase key requirements to reference the spec's actual R5 ('LoadGlobal/StoreGlobal MIR instructions') rather than code-level VariableClass behavior. Alternatively, clarify these as 'implementation observations' rather than 'key requirements'.

issues.description: hir-to-mir.md currently has NO existing requirement covering __name__ initialization. None of R1-R9 address module-level variable preamble setup. The spec modification will need to ADD a new requirement (e.g., R10) for 'Module preamble: inject StoreGlobal(__name__, "__main__") at the top of lower_top_level() after mb_register_builtins'. The reference context's key requirements section describes implementation details rather than flagging that a new spec requirement must be authored.
issues.recommendation: Note in the change spec that R10 (or next available R) must be added to hir-to-mir.md, not just that existing requirements are modified.
